blurb:
Josie has floppy ears, a nubby little tail, and enough energy to outlast most people on a weekend. She's a six-year-old hound mix, 35 pounds, and her foster puts her energy level at a 7 or 8 out of 10. She was adopted once before, but the match wasn't right for that family's pace. She's not for everyone, but for the right person, she's going to be a dream.

Here's what makes her worth the trip: she matches every bit of that energy with cuddle level. Her foster said it just like that. She wants to run, play, and go on adventures with you, and then she wants to crash on the couch together afterward. She's also sweet and affectionate, and by the end of her first shelter bath, she was giving kisses.

She does best as an only dog and shouldn't live with cats. She's got some mouthy, playful energy during play, nothing aggressive, just enthusiastic, so kids 10 and up would probably be a better fit than little ones. If you're an active person or family looking for a dog who'll actually keep up with you, go meet Josie. She's at Mostly Mutts in Marietta. Visit mostlymutts.org to apply.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• A returned adoption plus a specific energy-level mismatch warning signals this dog has been overlooked and is overdue for the right match
• The 'adventure partner by day, couch companion by night' contrast is the most emotionally complete and shareable pitch in this group
• Previous return plus narrowed household fit — no cats, no young kids, only dog — suggests an extended wait that gives editors a clear urgency angle

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
Iceman was adopted from Mostly Mutts as a kitten, grew up in a home he loved, and then his family's situation changed. Now he's five years old, 11 pounds, and back at the rescue looking for a second chapter. He's been through this before and he's handling it just fine, which probably tells you something about his personality.

He's a hang-out cat. Not needy, not aloof. He likes being near his people and watching the world go by from wherever he's posted up. His foster is still getting to know him, but his previous family had him for five years, and cats that stick around that long tend to be pretty easy to live with.

He wasn't a fan of other male cats in his last home, so a female cat roommate would be a better fit if you're looking to pair him up. Kids are fine, he's been around them before and did great. Dogs are an unknown, but a calm meet-and-greet could sort that out. He's neutered, microchipped, fully vaccinated, and combo tested negative. Adoption fee is $175.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Call or email to ask about Iceman and set up a visit.
scoring_notes:
• Five years of proven home success makes him one of the lowest-risk adult cat adoptions in this group
• The 'loved for years, lost through no fault of his own' narrative is a reliable reader-empathy trigger
• Returned after a long-term placement suggests he has been waiting while fosters gather intel, lending quiet urgency to the feature

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
During her photo session, Ladybug made sure everyone knew she was there. While the photographer was focused on the other kittens, she stuck her paw out until someone paid attention to her. Two months old, about 2 pounds, and already completely clear on what she wants.

She came into Mostly Mutts from animal control with her siblings, spent time in a foster home with adult cats she got along with just fine, and has recently moved into the shelter's Kitty Aquarium while she waits for her person. She's social, playful, and litter trained already, which is ahead of schedule for a kitten her size.

She'd be happy in a home with another cat, especially one who wouldn't mind a small, energetic shadow. Kids are welcome as long as they're gentle with a kitten this tiny. Dogs are an unknown. She still needs to finish her kitten shot series and will need to be spayed as she grows, both of which are required as part of the adoption. She's already microchipped.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Call or email to ask about Ladybug and go meet her at the shelter.
scoring_notes:
• Two-pound kitten with a personality outsized enough to demand a photographer's attention is instant newsletter gold
• Already litter trained and socially confident ahead of schedule makes her a genuinely easy adopt for a wide audience
• Recently moved into the shelter floor after foster care, suggesting a shorter wait and lower urgency relative to other candidates

blurb:
Jacquelyn came in from animal control, landed in a foster home, and promptly made herself at home with three other dogs. She's about two years old, 58 pounds, and her foster describes her as lovable, silly, and sweet. She's also a velcro dog, meaning she will find a way to be wherever you are and get her fair share of the affection. She's learned to dial it back when asked, though, so she's not chaos, just enthusiastic.

She does have a congenital kidney defect, which sounds scarier than it is. Her foster says it's easily managed and hasn't slowed her down at all. She just needs a few potty breaks during the day, no more than a typical dog, and she's been solid through the night. She's living fully and just wants someone to do that with.

She'd do best in a home where someone is around during the day and would love a friendly dog companion. Cats aren't her thing, and she hasn't been around toddlers, so meet-and-greet recommended if you have little ones. Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Head to their website to start an application and get a visit scheduled.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• A managed medical condition paired with a glowing foster report reframes a potential barrier as a compelling underdog story
• 'Living fully despite the odds' is the kind of narrative that converts casual readers into applicants
• Came in from animal control with no prior backstory, and household restrictions likely extend her wait, adding quiet urgency

shelter_name: Mostly Mutts Animal Rescue & Adoption, Inc.
blurb:
She came in as part of a litter called the Go-Gos, got adopted, and then life threw her a curveball. Gina is about 11 months old, barely 5 pounds, and has some ongoing stomach issues that sent her back to the rescue after just a month in her first home. The vet visits haven't turned up a clear answer yet, so this is just part of who she is. Her foster says she's the best cat ever, and honestly, the way she describes her, it's hard to argue.

Gina is independent but will absolutely curl up on you when she's in the mood. She's playful and sweet, and because she's so small she's basically stayed in permanent kitten mode. If you've always wanted a cat that never quite loses that kitten energy, she's genuinely that cat.

Because she's on a special diet, a home where she's the only pet would be the simplest setup. That said, her foster says she's done fine with the adult cats there, so if you have another cat and you're willing to feed them separately, there's room to make it work. She met a leashed dog without flinching, so a calm, cat-friendly dog could work too. She's liked the kids she's met, as long as they're gentle.

Gina is at Mostly Mutts in Kennesaw. Reach out by phone or email to start the process and set up a meet.
scoring_notes:
• Returned-adopter status and special diet requirements narrow the ideal home but create an emotionally resonant hook for the right reader
• The 'permanent kitten' angle paired with a foster's glowing endorsement gives editors a ready-made headline
• Returned after only a month signals recent re-entry; wait time is modest but the second-chance urgency adds newsletter appeal

blurb:
Yasmeen is eight years old, 58 pounds, and has already done the work. She went through a prison foster training program, spent time in a foster home, and her foster mom is out here saying people will hit the jackpot with her. That's not marketing copy. That's a foster who has lived with this dog and means it.

She was a little guarded when she first arrived, which makes sense given where she came from. But she opens up fast once she trusts you, and it only took a day in her training program for that to happen. She's now the kind of dog who is deeply loyal and loving to her people. She's good with cats and has warmed up to other dogs after an initial size-up period. She does like to be aware of who's coming into her space, so meeting new people outside first is recommended, something she's gotten much better at.

She'd do best with dog-savvy older kids, 15 and up, given her protective instincts. But if you want a calm, loving, loyal companion who's already trained and just needs a home to settle into, Yasmeen is exactly that dog. She's at Mostly Mutts in Marietta. Go to mostlymutts.org to start the process and ask to meet her with a trainer present.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• A prison training program graduate with a foster calling her a jackpot is the strongest human-interest hook in this entire set
• At eight years old with protective instincts and a recommended trainer-assisted meet, she faces the highest adoption headwinds, making her story the most newsworthy
• Age, behavioral nuance, and a layered placement history all point to the longest shelter journey, making her the clearest candidate for urgent, high-visibility feature placement
